*   **High-Level Data Warehouse Architecture:**
    *   Application Domain Layer: Air Canada, Syncrude, CPR/TTX, DND
    *   Lead User Layer: Air Canada, Syncrude, CPR/TTX, DND (aligned with application domains)
    *   Data Layer:
        *   "test machines" connecting to Databases DB 1, DB 2, ..., DB n
        *   "client 1 subnet", "client 2 subnet", "client n subnet"
        *   "isolation" blocks placed as interface between databases and clients subnets
    *   Projects Layer: IDS, ADAM, IO2, WILDMiner, FOSPHM/IGTE

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   DB 1, DB 2, DB n

title: 08-2-3 Sylvain Letourneau - Applications of Data Mining in Modern Maintenance
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 15]
**Data:**

*   6 years of maintenance and APU sensor data
*   5 years for model building, 1 year for evaluation.
*   69 occurrences of starter replacements over first 5 years (64 from A320, 5 from A319)
*   17 occurrences of starter replacements in the remaining year

**Results (on testing data):**

*   A320: Models generated alerts for 11 occurrences out of 12 (total 113 alerts, 5 potential false alerts)
*   A319: Models generated alerts for 2 occurrences out of 5 (total 61 alerts, 27 good, 34 to be validated)

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

title: 08-2-3 Sylvain Letourneau - Applications of Data Mining in Modern Maintenance
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 19]
Here's the technical content extracted from the image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Over 1 year, AC 217 consumes about $207000 more in fuel than AC 223 (assuming 5hr cruising per day and $0.31/kg of fuel)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Name:** ESTIMATED FUEL CONSUMPTION BY ENGINE (1997)
*   **Axes:**
    *   X-axis: Engine Serial Number
    *   Y-axis: Fuel (Kg)
*   **Curves:**
    *   Bars representing fuel consumption for different engine serial numbers.
*   **Key Values (Approximate from the graph):**
    *   Engine Serial Numbers: 731542, 731359, 731654 - high consumption (close to 2.4x10^6 Kg)
    *   Engine Serial Numbers: 731335, 731631, 731250 - low consumption (close to 2.1x10^6 Kg)
*   **Legend:**
    *   FINS, 217, 223

**3. Tables:**
NO_TABLE_DETECTED

**4. Diagrams:**
NO_DIAGRAM_DETECTED

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, and Parameters:**
*   EGT (Exhaust Gas Temperature)
*   Fuel Flow
*   N1 vib (Fan Speed Vibration)
*   N2 vib Turbine Speed Vibration
*   $0.31/kg (Cost of fuel per kilogram)
*   5 hr (Cruising time per day assumption)
*   Data - 5 years of Engine Cruise Reports
